record.field: Outline is a fast, real-time collaborative knowledge base and team wiki you host on your own infrastructure.

Outline is a fast, real-time collaborative knowledge base and team wiki you host on your own infrastructure.

Built with React and Node.js, Outline gives teams a Markdown-native space to write documentation, runbooks and internal wikis, with nested document collections, automatic backlinks and millisecond full-text search. It feels like a premium SaaS tool such as Notion or Confluence, but every byte of data stays under your control. Self-hosting requires PostgreSQL, Redis and an external identity provider for sign-in, so it suits teams with some dev-ops capability.

Key features
  • Real-time collaborative editor with Markdown shortcuts, slash commands and media embeds
  • Nested document collections with automatic backlinks and fast full-text search
  • Granular access control: read/write permissions, user groups, guest users and public sharing
  • SSO via external identity providers (Google, Slack, GitLab, Discord, Azure, generic OIDC)
  • Integrations with Slack, Figma, Loom and a documented REST API for automation
  • Localization in 20+ languages with right-to-left support, plus Markdown export with no lock-in
Best for

Growing teams and organizations that want a privacy-focused, self-hosted internal wiki or documentation platform with real-time collaboration, and that already use (or can deploy) an SSO identity provider.

field.name: tag_ids
field.eval: [Command.set([ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_self_hosted'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_documentation'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_notes'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_collaboration'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_productivity'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_search'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_cms')])]

record.field: services: outline: image: outlinewiki/outline:0.82.0 restart: always depends_on: - postgres - redis - dex ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:3000' environment: NODE_ENV: production URL: {{ url }} FORCE_HTTPS: 'false' SECRET_KEY: {{ secret_key }} UTILS_SECRET: {{ utils_secret }} DATABASE_URL: postgres://outline:{{ postgres_password }}@postgres:5432/outline PGSSLMODE: disable REDIS_URL: redis://redis:6379 OIDC_CLIENT_ID: outline OIDC_CLIENT_SECRET: {{ client_secret }} OIDC_AUTH_URI: {{ dex_url }}/auth OIDC_TOKEN_URI: {{ dex_url }}/token OIDC_USERINFO_URI: {{ dex_url }}/userinfo container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network dex: image: ghcr.io/dexidp/dex:v2.37.0 restart: always volumes: - ../files/etc/dex/config.yaml:/etc/dex/config.yaml command: - dex - serve - /etc/dex/config.yaml ports: - 5556 container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_dex' networks: - internal postgres: image: postgres:15 restart: always environment: POSTGRES_DB: outline POSTGRES_USER: outline POSTGRES_PASSWORD: {{ postgres_password }} volumes: - ./postgres_data_test_outline_khufpx:/var/lib/postgresql/data container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_postgres' networks: - internal redis: image: redis:latest restart: always command: redis-server --appendonly yes volumes: - ./redis_data_test_outline_khufpx:/data container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_redis' networks: - internal networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump consistente de PostgreSQL (servicio en marcha) → $BACKUP_DATA_DIR docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c 'pg_dump -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --no-owner -Fc' \ > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump" echo "pg_dump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Levanta solo la DB, espera readiness y restaura docker compose up -d postgres for i in $(seq 1 30); do docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c 'pg_isready -U "$POSTGRES_USER"' && break sleep 2 done docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c 'pg_restore -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --clean --if-exists --no-owner' \ < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.dump" # Verificación: la DB no quedó vacía (sh -c con dobles comillas; $VAR del contenedor con \$) N=$(docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c "psql -U \$POSTGRES_USER -d \$POSTGRES_DB -tAc \"SELECT count(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema='public'\"") echo "tablas restauradas: $N" test "$N" -gt 0

issuer: http://${dex_domain}
web: http: 0.0.0.0:5556
storage: type: memory
enablePasswordDB: true
frontend: issuer: Outline
logger: level: debug
staticPasswords: - email: "admin@example.com" # bcrypt hash of the string "password": $(echo password | htpasswd -BinC 10 admin | cut -d: -f2) hash: "$2y$10$jsRWHw54uxTUIfhjgUrB9u8HSzPk7TUuQri9sXZrKzRXcScvwYor." username: "admin" userID: "1"
oauth2: skipApprovalScreen: true alwaysShowLoginScreen: false passwordConnector: local
staticClients: - id: "outline" redirectURIs: - http://${main_domain}/auth/oidc.callback name: "Outline" secret: "${client_secret}"
